architecture.execution_mode: argv-array only; shell false by default and denied unless exception token names policy authority
architecture.failure_learning: timeout, nonzero, denied method, forbidden arg, path escape, output overflow, and spawn failure emit structured failure event
architecture.kill_semantics: on timeout: terminate process, bounded wait, kill escalation if still alive, classify timeout failure
architecture.method_reliability: must load FORBIDDEN_VALIDATION_METHODS_v1 and METHOD_RELIABILITY_ROUTER_POLICY_v1 before execution
architecture.must_emit: JSON decision packet plus optional failure event
architecture.retry_budget: bounded max_attempts; no infinite loops; retry only for retryable classes
architecture.time_budget: per_attempt_timeout_sec and total_timeout_sec required; default critical cap 30s total unless narrower policy applies

decision: Create a single governed subprocess primitive that all future shell/process operations must use before direct subprocess, bash, zip/unzip, sha256sum, find, node, or python invocations are permitted inside OS-managed workflows.
design_constraints: No direct string shell command in governed workflows.
No unbounded timeout or retry count.
No cwd outside canonical runtime root unless explicit path authority allows it.
No inherited broad environment by default; use minimal env overlay.
No stdout/stderr unbounded capture; byte caps required.
Forbidden validation methods block before spawn.
name: bounded_subprocess_wrapper
problem: MetaBlooms subprocess-like operations currently have inconsistent timeout, retry, receipt, denylist, and failure-learning enforcement.
